channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Turning GitHub Copilot into a “Best Practices Coach” with Copilot Spaces + a Markdown Knowledge Base
item.description: Why Copilot Spaces + Markdown repos work so well When you ask Copilot generic questions (“How should we log errors?” “What’s our API versioning approach?”), the model will often respond with reasonable defaults. But reasonable defaults are not the same as your standards. Copilot Spaces solve the...
item.dc:creator: mohashaikh

item.title: Quick question about window PC requirements for meta link cable?
item.description: Do I not have the right requirements? I finally got myself a link cable and was going to connect it to my computer, but it’s just not reading it and it’s saying, I don’t meet the updated minimum specifications. I have a: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 Laptop GPU (sorry for being dumb I am)
item.dc:creator: Paulload

item.title: Is it possible to run ryujinx canary on an administrator account on windows?
item.description: hi! I recently saw a tutorial for opening ryujinx (from extracted folder) when it isn't working, and the guys said that yu need to make a local acc for your pc. I'm wondering if there's anyway I can do this without doing that, because I'd have to redownload everything.
item.dc:creator: Manstaway

item.title: Why Windows 11 still depends on 1990s code
item.description: Windows 11 looks modern on the surface. Rounded corners, AI features, redesigned menus, cloud integration, Copilot branding everywhere. Underneath all of that, Microsoft is still relying on core technology designed during the Windows 95 era.That is not speculation or internet nostalgia. Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: Zillmann

item.title: CHERIoT-Ibex: Closing the door on memory safety vulnerabilities with hardware-enforced protection
item.description: Memory safety vulnerabilities—largely arising from widely used programming languages such as C and C++—remain a leading cause of exploitable software defects across systems, from embedded devices to cloud-scale infrastructure. In simple terms, memory safety ensures that software accesses only the...
item.dc:creator: kunyanliu

item.title: What's New in Microsoft Entra: May 2026
item.description: Welcome to the May edition of our monthly newsletter, summarizing the latest news and developments in the exciting, ever-evolving world of Microsoft Entra. What went into General Availability (GA) since April 2026? Network content filtering by file type in Global Secure Access - Microsoft Global...
item.dc:creator: Martin_Coetzer

item.title: Generate Embeddings Function and External Model Object Support Are Now Generally Available in Azure SQL
item.description: We are excited to announce the General Availability (GA) of AI_GENERATE_EMBEDDINGS and CREATE EXTERNAL MODEL in Azure SQL Database and Azure SQL Managed Instance. These two T-SQL features — CREATE EXTERNAL MODEL and AI_GENERATE_EMBEDDINGS — work together as a single, integrated pipeline for...
item.dc:creator: Aaron Saidi

item.title: Announcing the public preview of the Microsoft 365 Copilot Agent Evaluations tool
item.description: The Microsoft 365 Copilot Agent Evaluations tool helps developers measure and improve the quality of agents they build for Microsoft 365 Copilot. The post Announcing the public preview of the Microsoft 365 Copilot Agent Evaluations tool appeared first on Microsoft 365 Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Barry Gervin, Sai Deepthi Kovvuru

item.title: Introducing the Azure Resource Manager MCP Server!
item.description: We're super excited to announce the public preview of the Azure Resource Manager MCP Server! This is a remote MCP server that provides tools to give AI agents first-class access to Azure infrastructure operations through Azure Resource Manager (ARM). AI agents can now be equipped with tools to...
item.dc:creator: stevenbucher

item.title: Released: May 2026 Exchange Server Hotfix Update
item.description: Microsoft has released Hotfix Update (HU) for: Exchange Server Subscription Edition (SE) HU is available for the following specific version of Exchange Server: Exchange SE RTM The May 2026 HU does not contain any new Exchange Server security updates but contains new functionality. Please see the...
item.dc:creator: The_Exchange_Team

item.title: Upcoming deprecation of Grok Code Fast 1
item.description: We will deprecate Grok Code Fast 1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences (including Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions) on May 15th: Model Deprecation date… The post Upcoming deprecation of Grok Code Fast 1 appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot code review comment types now in usage metrics API
item.description: To help you understand the activity generated by Copilot code review users, the Copilot usage metrics API now breaks down Copilot code review suggestions by comment type. A new copilot_suggestions_by_comment_type… The post Copilot code review comment types now in usage metrics API appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Inspektor Gadget Completes Its First Independent Security Audit
item.description: One thing I've learned working with Open Source software over the years is that the projects you can trust most are the ones willing to let someone else test and review. That's what's recently happened with Inspektor Gadget, the open source eBPF tool for Kubernetes observability and Linux host...
item.dc:creator: Brian Benz

item.title: Retirement of Direct Exchange ActiveSync Certificate-Based Authentication by End of 2026
item.description: We are announcing the deprecation of Exchange ActiveSync (EAS) certificate-based authentication (CBA) directly to Exchange Online. By the end of 2026, Microsoft will no longer support direct CBA connections from EAS mobile email clients to Exchange Online. After that date, any EAS clients using CBA...
item.dc:creator: The_Exchange_Team

item.title: Introducing cert-manager for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes: now in Public Preview
item.description: Today we’re releasing a public preview of cert-manager for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes. It’s an Arc extension that automates TLS certificate and trust bundle management for edge Kubernetes clusters. If you’re running Kubernetes at the edge: in factories, retail stores, remote sites, you’ve...
item.dc:creator: NicolasMondada

item.title: Active attack: Dirty Frag Linux vulnerability expands post-compromise risk
item.description: Dirty Frag is a newly disclosed Linux local privilege escalation vulnerability affecting kernel networking and memory-fragment handling components including esp4, esp6, and rxrpc. The vulnerability enables reliable escalation from an unprivileged user to root and may be leveraged after initial...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Why age assurance laws matter for developers
item.description: Youth safety requirements are moving down the tech stack to operating systems and app stores—raising new questions for open source developers. The post Why age assurance laws matter for developers appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Margaret Tucker

item.title: Safely Migrating Terraform Managed Disks on Azure Using Stable Keys and Copilot
item.description: The Root Cause: Index-Based "for_each" Keys: Many Terraform modules flatten VM and disk definitions into a list and use the list index as the for_each key: for_each = { for index, sp in local.managed_disks : index => sp } This pattern looks harmless, but the index is not stable: Adding a disk to...
item.dc:creator: shwetayadav

item.title: Secure Medallion Architecture Pattern on Azure Databricks (Part II)
item.description: Disclaimer: The views in this article are my own and do not represent Microsoft or Databricks. This article is part of a series focused on deploying a secure Medallion Architecture. The series follows a top-down approach , beginning with a high-level architectural perspective and gradually drilling...
item.dc:creator: mscagliola

item.title: How researchers are using GitHub Innovation Graph data to reveal the “digital complexity” of nations
item.description: Researchers share in an interview how they used GitHub data to predict GDP, inequality, and emissions in ways that traditional economic data misses, along with our Q4 2025 data release. The post How researchers are using GitHub Innovation Graph data to reveal the “digital complexity” of nations...
item.dc:creator: Kevin Xu

item.title: Update Your Exchange SE Hybrid On-premises Rich Coexistence to Graph
item.description: About a year ago, we announced Exchange Server Security Changes for Hybrid Deployments. This change impacted Exchange hybrid customers who host some of their mailboxes on-premises and their on-premises users need access to “rich coexistence” features (Free/Busy lookups, MailTips and profile picture...
item.dc:creator: The_Exchange_Team

item.title: General Availability of Mailbox Import and Export Microsoft Graph APIs
item.description: As a part of our continuing march to Exchange Web Service (EWS) deprecation in Exchange Online (see Exchange Online EWS, Your Time is Almost Up | Microsoft Community Hub) - the Microsoft Graph Team announced another milestone in enabling developers to efficiently manage, migrate, and integrate...
item.dc:creator: Nino_Bilic

item.title: Disable commit comments on the user level
item.description: You can now choose whether commit comments are enabled or disabled by default for repositories owned by your personal account. This makes it easier to manage commit comment behavior across… The post Disable commit comments on the user level appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: More flexible secrets and variables for Copilot cloud agent
item.description: When you delegate a task to Copilot cloud agent, it works in the background in its own development environment powered by GitHub Actions. You can pass secrets and variables to… The post More flexible secrets and variables for Copilot cloud agent appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The Windows 11 Photos app cannot display the full image when viewing landscape-oriented photos
item.description: I just started using Windows 11, and I noticed that when I run a slideshow in the new Photos app, landscape-oriented images aren’t displayed in full (part of the image is automatically cropped), while portrait-oriented images display normally. Is this a settings issue? How can I fix it?
item.dc:creator: Michael77

item.title: Windows 11 causes discrete graphics cards to be locked at their base clock speed when idle
item.description: Testing has revealed that this is not a driver issue, but rather a problem caused by Windows updates. After comparing multiple versions, this issue was observed only in the latest version, 8246. Specifically, the latest update causes the discrete graphics card to enter sleep mode too quickly. When...
item.dc:creator: BladeCipher

item.title: In Windows 11 Insider Preview 26300.8346, the Start button is offset to the left and misaligned
item.description: Submit a bug report: In Windows 11 Insider Preview build 26300.8346, the Start button is shifted to the left and does not align with the taskbar.
item.dc:creator: bonirl

item.title: The Windows 10 update failed, so I'm planning to switch to Windows 11
item.description: Due to issues with the Windows 10 version, I’m unable to install ME and updates keep failing, so I plan to install Windows 11 directly. The available editions are Home, Pro, Enterprise, and Workstation. I primarily use video editing software like Premiere Pro, After Effects, and Photoshop, as well...
item.dc:creator: JacksonRobinson

item.title: Using the Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK to Update User Profiles
item.description: Now rolling out to Microsoft 365 tenants is the ability to update user profile cards with details of awards and certifications held by users. Usually, HR departments would provide the source data and probably arrange for the data to be ingested into the Graph through a Copilot connector, but it’s...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Someone please help me - Windows Insider Program
item.description: Guys can someone pls tell me like should I buy asus or loq 4050 or 3050 laptop and also one thing which site to use like lenovo or flipkkart or amazon and my mom don't wanna use credit cards cuz and she always opposedit so how can we actually buy on emi pls help
item.dc:creator: euincy

item.title: How to Reduce No-Shows for WooCommerce Appointments
item.description: A no-show isn't just a missed appointment; it's a slot you could have given to someone else, revenue you planned around, and time you can't recover. For salons, clinics, coaches, and any service business running on a fixed schedule, even a handful of no-shows a week adds up to a meaningful revenue...
item.dc:creator: Nerfft

item.title: Windows 11 Insider Program Download Update Beta Channel stuck at 99%
item.description: I already tried to restart my PC multiple times, reconnect wifi, clearing update cache, running DISM (it came out clean), stopping and restart Windows Update services, and simply waiting overnight, but it still stuck at 99% no matter what I do. I then tried to switch to Release channel and there...
item.dc:creator: Tracecea

item.title: CodeQL 2.25.3 adds Swift 6.3 support
item.description: CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. We’ve recently released CodeQL 2.25.3, which adds support for Swift 6.3,… The post CodeQL 2.25.3 adds Swift 6.3 support appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Why deleting files does not free up space on mac
item.description: I'm a bit of a Mac beginner and run into a confusing storage issue. I spent the morning deleting about 10GB of old video projects and downloads because my disk was almost full. I made sure to empty the Trash, but when I go to About This Mac , the available space is exactly the same as it was before...
item.dc:creator: Yahyati

item.title: How to delete or clear cache on macbook air automatically
item.description: Hi everyone!I just noticed my Mac has been feeling a little sluggish lately, and a few of my apps aren't loading content as quickly as they used to. After doing some reading online, I saw several suggestions to clear cache on mac to help improve performance and free up some space, but as a newer...
item.dc:creator: Mustafoan

item.title: Announcing Microsoft Host Integration Server 2028: Modern connectivity for IBM Mainframes Midranges
item.description: Many organizations continue to rely on IBM mainframe and midrange platforms for high-value, mission-critical workloads. At the same time, application modernization, security requirements, and hybrid cloud operating models are driving the need for reliable, supported connectivity between host...
item.dc:creator: hcamposu

item.title: From Test Cases to Trust: Elevating Enterprise Quality with GitHub Copilot
item.description: The Traditional QA Bottleneck In complex enterprise systems, QA teams often face familiar challenges: Time‑consuming test case creation from evolving requirements Repetitive automation scripting and refactoring Heavy regression cycles under tight release timelines Limited bandwidth for deeper risk...
item.dc:creator: SagarPatra

item.title: Upcoming deprecation of GPT-4.1
item.description: We will deprecate the following model across all GitHub Copilot experiences (including Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions) on 6/1/2026: Model Deprecation date Suggested alternative… The post Upcoming deprecation of GPT-4.1 appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Improving token efficiency in GitHub Agentic Workflows
item.description: Agentic workflows that run on every pull request can quietly accumulate large API bills. Here's how we instrumented our own production workflows, found the inefficiencies, and built agents to fix them. The post Improving token efficiency in GitHub Agentic Workflows appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Landon Cox

item.title: Claude Sonnet 4 deprecated
item.description: We have deprecated the following model across all GitHub Copilot experiences (including Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions) on May 6, 2026. Model Deprecation date… The post Claude Sonnet 4 deprecated appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Announcing general availability of the mailbox import and export Microsoft Graph APIs
item.description: The mailbox import and export APIs on Microsoft Graph are now generally available. The post Announcing general availability of the mailbox import and export Microsoft Graph APIs appeared first on Microsoft 365 Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Graph team

item.title: Public Preview: Migrate your regional virtual machines to availability zones
item.description: This new capability enables you to move your existing regional (nonzonal) VMs and VMSS Flex deployments into specific availability zones while preserving the VM names, data disks, and other stateful properties. We're excited to announce the public preview of regional to zonal migration for Azure...
item.dc:creator: micahmckittrick

item.title: Least privilege AI agents: A new azd template from Curity and Microsoft
item.description: If you ever built an AI agent demo, you probably had this moment. Everything works: the agent interprets natural language, calls the right tools, and returns the right data. Then you start designing for the real users of the app. You think about data boundaries: what if someone tries to get data...
item.dc:creator: Kristen Womack

item.title: Enterprise Live Migrations is now in public preview
item.description: Enterprise Live Migrations (ELM) is now available in public preview. ELM gives enterprise administrators a new way to migrate repositories from GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) to GitHub Enterprise Cloud with… The post Enterprise Live Migrations is now in public preview appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: When prompts become shells: RCE vulnerabilities in AI agent frameworks
item.description: New research exposes how prompt injection in AI agent frameworks can lead to remote code execution. Learn how these vulnerabilities work, what’s impacted, and how to secure your agents. The post When prompts become shells: RCE vulnerabilities in AI agent frameworks appeared first on Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team, Uri Oren, Amit Eliahu and Dor Edry

item.title: Agent pull requests are everywhere. Here’s how to review them.
item.description: A practical guide to reviewing agent-generated pull requests: what to look for, where issues hide, and how to catch technical debt before it ships. The post Agent pull requests are everywhere. Here’s how to review them. appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Griffiths

item.title: Tableau to Power BI Migration: Semantic Layer-First Approach for Cloud Architects
item.description: Author's: Mahjabin Ahmed, Yassine El Ouardi, Lavanya Sreedhar LavanyaSreedhar​, Peter Lo PeterLo​, Aryan Anmol aryananmol​, Shreya Harvu shreyaharvu​ and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ In this guide, we provide practical guidance for migrating from Tableau to Power BI, with a focus on technical best...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: Execute DAX Queries REST API (Preview)
item.description: Author: Kay Unkroth - Principal Program Manager
item.dc:creator: kayu

item.title: What’s New in Aspire 13.3
item.description: Aspire 13.3 is here with the Aspireify skill, command results, browser logs, Kubernetes and AKS deployment, and more. The post What’s New in Aspire 13.3 appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Montaquila

item.title: Copilot Studio gets faster with .NET 10 on WebAssembly
item.description: Microsoft Copilot Studio recently upgraded its .NET WebAssembly engine to .NET 10. The migration was straightforward, simplified deployment, and delivered another round of meaningful performance gains for end users. The post Copilot Studio gets faster with .NET 10 on WebAssembly appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Daniel Roth
